headlines.title: Jewish Democrats grapple with a changing party and Israel’s entrenched leadership
headlines.description: Onstage and on the sidelines of the recent Jewish Democratic Council of America Leadership Summit in Washington, prominent pro-Israel Democrats tiptoed into one of the biggest debates roiling their party

headlines.title: Reality check: Spencer Pratt seeks to convert online attention to real-life votes in L.A.
headlines.description: The viral, AI-generated video opens with the iconic Hollywood sign on fire and features Los Angeles Mayor Karen Bass wearing makeup to look like the Joker.

headlines.title: Iran says no deal ‘imminent’ despite progress in talks with U.S.
headlines.description: Iran warned Monday that an agreement to end the war with the United States was not imminent, after President Donald Trump raised and then lowered expectations that a deal may be close

headlines.title: What we know about the suspect killed and bystander wounded in shooting near the White House
headlines.description: The White House was on lockdown after dozens of gunshots were heard outside Saturday evening.

headlines.title: Trump says he won’t ‘rush’ into a deal with Iran, softening hopes of an imminent agreement
headlines.description: President Donald Trump said he won’t “rush into a deal” to end the U.S.-Israeli war with Iran after senior Republican lawmakers warned it could be a “disastrous mistake.”

headlines.title: Trump Says He Told Negotiators 'Not to Rush Into' a Deal With Iran
headlines.description: The Trump administration and Iran both say progress is being made to end the war but there’s no deal yet. President Donald Trump says he’s told his representatives “not to rush into a deal,” while an Iran spokesman said, “No one can claim an agreement is about to be signed imminently.” The key terms at issue are Iran’s nuclear program and the fate of nearly 1,000lbs of highly enriched uranium, and control over the Strait of Hormuz. NBC’s Richard Engel reports for TODAY.

headlines.title: New Details Emerge on Man Who Opened Fire on Secret Service
headlines.description: New details are emerging about the 21-year-old man who officials say walked up to a Secret Service checkpoint Saturday evening, pulled a pistol out of his bag and began shooting at officers who returned fire, killing him. The suspect was known to Secret Service and was arrested in 2025 for attempting to gain entry to White House grounds. Five senior law enforcement officials say he had a history of mental health concerns. NBC’s Julie Tsirkin reports for TODAY.

headlines.title: Trump says he won't 'rush into' deal to end war with Iran
headlines.description: President Trump said Sunday that he will not “rush into a deal” to end the ongoing U.S.-Israeli war with Iran, after senior Republican lawmakers warned it could be a “disastrous mistake.” It comes a day after the president said that a potential deal was “largely negotiated.” NBC News’ Raf Sanchez reports.

headlines.title: Suicides rise in ICE detention; 911 calls detail serious cases of self-harm
headlines.description: Suicides rise in ICE detention; 911 calls detail serious cases of self-harm

headlines.title: Extended Interview: Tom Llamas sits down with Secretary of State Marco Rubio
headlines.description: On the sidelines of the Trump-Xi Summit in Beijing, NBC News' Tom Llamas sits down with Secretary of State Marco Rubio to discuss China policy, the war with Iran, the future of Cuba, and more.

headlines.title: Map: How much you pay in federal and state gas taxes
headlines.description: As national gas price averages head toward $5 per gallon, one state has already suspended its gas tax, and now President Donald Trump and his Cabinet are talking about doing the same.

headlines.title: Rep. Thomas Massie warns GOP will be ‘very vulnerable’ in the midterms
headlines.description: Rep. Thomas Massie, R-Ky., warned on “Meet the Press” that the Republican Party will be “very vulnerable” during the midterm elections.
